page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32-lssd is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 120 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture, the server provides 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz. The CPU allocation is dedicated, supported by a substantial 504 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. The hardware configuration includes 1745 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 5 local SSDs, with no integrated GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU performance, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression scoring in the top 10% tier. Multi-core performance is strong to average, with Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU scores falling in the top 25% tier, while stress-ng multi-core performance sits in the middle 50% tier. Memory bandwidth tests for both read and write operations consistently register in the average tier. The processor's advanced instruction support, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), enables strong performance in cryptography and text processing, alongside top-tier text and image processing capabilities.

With 1745 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage and a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio of 3.75 GB per core, this instance is optimized for workloads requiring fast local storage and high single-threaded compute efficiency. It is well-suited for database operations, web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference, where it achieves strong text generation speeds for small and medium models. The dedicated CPU allocation and high-speed local storage provide a reliable resource density for data-intensive applications, balancing compute power with high-speed local I/O without the cost overhead of GPU hardware.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32-lssd is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs, 120 GB of RAM, and 1745 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Powered by a 2.3 GHz Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with 16 physical cores and a 504 MB L3 cache, this server delivers top-tier single-core performance, particularly in image processing, text processing, and single-threaded compression. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain strong to average. The hardware supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which enhance cryptographic and matrix operations. Lacking a GPU, it relies on its high-speed local storage and compute density to handle workloads such as database operations, static web serving, and small-to-medium LLM inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32-lssd is an x86_64 server with 32 vCPUs, 120 GB RAM, and 1745 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core CPU and text processing performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-32-lssd features 32 vCPUs, 120 GB RAM, and 1745 GB NVMe SSD storage for high-performance compute workloads.
tagline: A compute-optimized server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed local NVMe SSD storage, and top-tier single-core performance.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs and 120 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor.
1745 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage bundled across 5 drives.
Top-tier single-core performance in image processing, text processing, and compression.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated cryptography and matrix calculations.
Optimized for database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference.
